architecture.description: A web application that displays current weather, a 5-day forecast, and a 3-hourly forecast for a specified location, utilizing the OpenWeatherMap API via a proxy backend.

endpoints.description: Proxies the OpenWeatherMap API to fetch current weather data for a given location (latitude, longitude, or city name).
Proxies the OpenWeatherMap API to fetch 5-day / 3-hourly forecast data for a given location (latitude, longitude, or city name).
Proxies a geocoding service (e.g., OpenWeatherMap Geocoding) to search for location coordinates by city name.

pages.name: Home
pages.description: Main page displaying current weather, 5-day forecast, and 3-hourly forecast based on user input or browser geolocation.

assumptions: Target platform is a web application.
Application will display current weather, a 5-day forecast, and a 3-hourly forecast.
UI/UX will be clean, modern, and responsive, with no specific styling preferences beyond that.
OpenWeatherMap API will be used for weather data.
A minimal Node.js/Express backend will serve as an API proxy to protect the API key.
Frontend will be built using React.js.
No database is required as user authentication, saved locations, or historical data are not in scope.
No user authentication or account management features will be included.
Location input will support city name search and browser geolocation for current location.
Units of measurement will default to Metric (Celsius, m/s) with an option to switch to Imperial (Fahrenheit, mph).
No monetization strategy or specific business goals are assumed, focusing solely on core functionality.
Deployment environment is assumed to be a standard cloud platform (e.g., Vercel for frontend, Render for backend).
